question: Which two cases involved suspiciously generous employment offers, and what hidden purpose did each offer serve?
question_interpretation: Identify the two stories in which a character was offered unusually high pay for simple or bizarre work, and explain the hidden criminal motives behind these offers.
reference_answer: The two cases are 'The Red-Headed League' and 'The Adventure of the Copper Beeches'. In 'The Red-Headed League', Jabez Wilson was paid £4 a week to copy the Encyclopaedia Britannica, serving the hidden purpose of keeping him away from his shop so John Clay could dig a tunnel into a bank vault. In 'The Adventure of the Copper Beeches', Violet Hunter was offered £120 a year to act as a governess, serving the hidden purpose of forcing her to impersonate Alice Rucastle so that her fiancé would believe she had abandoned him.
